sn11.7:1.1: At Sāvatthī. "Bhikkhus, once in the past, when Sakka, lord of the devas, was alone in seclusion, the following reflection arose in his mind: ‘Though someone may be my sworn enemy, I should not transgress even against him.’
sn11.7:1.2: "Then, bhikkhus, Vepacitti, lord of the asuras, having known with his own mind the reflection in Sakka's mind, approached Sakka, lord of the devas. Sakka saw Vepacitti coming in the distance and said to him: ‘Stop, Vepacitti, you’re caught!’[n.622] Spk: As soon as Sakka said this, Vepacitti became as if bound by bonds on his four limbs and neck. -
sn11.7:2.1: ‘Dear sir, do not abandon the idea that just occurred to you.’[n.623] I read with Be: tadeva tvaṁ ma pajahasi. Ee1 reads pahasi, which gives the same sense, but Se and Ee2 have marisa pahasi, which yields the opposite meaning.
sn11.7:3.1: ‘Swear, Vepacitti, that you won’t transgress against me.’
